nutrient_source.title: Baked beef topside, lean — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo), record F06030002
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for baked beef topside, lean (cr_id F06030002, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat (0.9g), monounsaturated fat (1.1g), polyunsaturated fat (0.1g), and trans fat (0.1g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 13364 — Beef, round, top round, lean, cooked, roasted — as HPB does not separately report fatty acid fractions for this entry; this adaptation is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g per 100g)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database — not applicable; food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Baked beef topside, lean contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g. Pure protein and fat foods do not elicit a meaningful glycaemic response and are excluded from GI classification per University of Sydney GI Database methodology. Glycemic index is set to null (not 0) and gi_category is set to not_applicable. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. No GI measurement is required or available for this food class.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (54mg/100g) and high potassium (340mg/100g) yield a favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Baked lean beef topside is suitable for hypertensive individuals due to its very low sodium content (54mg/100g) and high potassium (340mg/100g); preparation should avoid added salt, salty marinades, or high-sodium gravies to preserve this favourable profile.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high protein (32.7g/100g) supports satiety and glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 0g carbohydrate and 32.7g protein per 100g, baked lean beef topside has no glycaemic impact and is an excellent protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes managing blood glucose; avoid high-sugar sauces or glazes during preparation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (2.8g/100g) and low saturated fat (~0.9g/100g); cholesterol 62mg/100g is moderate
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lean baked beef topside is a low-fat, low-saturated-fat protein source appropriate for cardiovascular disease management; cholesterol at 62mg/100g is moderate and within acceptable limits for most cardiac dietary guidelines when consumed in standard portions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat contains arachidonic acid (omega-6) which may promote pro-inflammatory cytokine production
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise red meat consumption including lean beef topside due to its pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid content; if consumed, limit to portions of 80g or less and pair with anti-inflammatory vegetables.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Baked beef topside, lean is a cooked red meat product derived from the rear leg round portion (topside cut) of beef cattle. The cut is prepared by dry-heat baking until fully cooked through, with all visible and separable internal and external fat removed, leaving only lean muscle meat. The result is a very high-protein (32.7g/100g), very low-fat (2.8g/100g) food with zero carbohydrate and zero sugar, making it one of the leanest cooked beef options available. The texture is firm and slightly dense due to the low fat content and dry cooking method; flavour is savoury and beefy without the richness associated with fattier cuts. Nutritionally, it provides 158 kcal per 100g, significant iron (2.4mg), potassium (340mg), and low sodium (54mg), making it suitable for hypertension and Type 2 Diabetes management. Cholesterol is moderate at 62mg/100g. As a red meat, it contains arachidonic acid and is not recommended in large quantities for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or inflammatory conditions. It is naturally gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal-compatible when sourced from halal-certified suppliers. Commonly served as a main protein component in Western-style meals, it pairs well with vegetables and low-sodium sauces.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F06030002, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved regional source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat and fatty acid breakdown estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy closest match (beef round top round lean cooked roasted, NDB 13364) and documented accordingly. All values are internally consistent and cross-validated between the two approved sources.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database — this food is a pure protein and fat product with no carbohydrate content (naturally zero). Sugar_g is naturally zero as there are no sugars present in lean cooked beef muscle meat. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is an animal muscle meat product containing no plant fibre. Saturated_fat_g (0.9g), monounsaturated_fat_g (1.1g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1g), and trans_fat_g (0.1g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 13364 (Beef, round, top round, lean, cooked, roasted) as HPB does not separately report fatty acid fractions for this entry; this is an approved secondary source adaptation. Fields set to -1 (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g): detailed vitamin and mineral data not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo for this specific record (F06030002) as of 2026-04-16; these values are not available in the primary source. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g/100g); GI set to null (not 0) and gi_category set to not_applicable per critical rule q4. This is a Western generic food item with no mandatory multilingual naming requirements under Project Veritas v2 rules; local_names fields are null by design. FoodEx2 code A0CKP assigned as closest defensible match for cooked lean beef topside; FoodOn URI assigned for beef topside food product class.
